Frieren: Beyond Journey's End (Japanese: 葬送のフリーレン, Hepburn: Sōsō no Furīren, rgh. "Frieren, the Final Farewell to the Dead") is a Japanese manga series written by Kanehito Yamada and illustrated by Tsukasa Abe. It has been serialized in Shogakukan's shōnen manga magazine Weekly Shōnen Sunday since April 2020, with its chapters collected in eleven tankōbon volumes as of September 2023. The series is licensed for English release in North America by Viz Media. An anime television series adaptation produced by Madhouse premiered in September 2023.

title: FRIEREN Staff from Frieren: Beyond Journey's End (Sousou no Frieren)
description:
Harness the Magic: 3D Model of Frieren's Staff from Frieren: Beyond Journey's End!

Embark on a mystical journey inspired by Frieren: Beyond Journey's End with our meticulously crafted replica of Frieren's staff, the iconic weapon wielded by the enigmatic character. With exquisite detail and precision, this staff captures the essence of Frieren's magical prowess and unwavering determination.

Key Features:
- Authentic Design: Our Frieren's Staff replica is meticulously designed to faithfully capture the intricate details of the original weapon as depicted in the anime and manga. From the ornate engravings to the graceful curves, every aspect reflects the magical essence of Frieren's staff.
- Modular Design for Easy Printing and Painting: The staff is intelligently divided into parts for convenient 3D printing and painting. Each part is carefully separated based on materials, allowing for ease of assembly and customization. Whether you're a seasoned enthusiast or new to 3D printing, our model ensures a smooth process.
- Adjustable Length: The staff's handle is designed with adjustable sections, allowing you to customize the length of the staff to your preference. Whether you seek precision in cosplay or versatility in display, our model provides flexibility to suit your needs.
- Built for Detail and Durability: Our staff model is crafted with both detail and durability in mind. Every contour and texture is faithfully replicated, ensuring a true-to-life representation of Frieren's weapon. Additionally, a 10mm diameter tube cavity inside the staff provides added reinforcement for enhanced durability.

Embrace the magic and mystery of Frieren's journey with our stunning replica of her staff. Whether for cosplay, display, or as a tribute to the captivating series, our model is sure to enchant fans and collectors alike.

title: Frieren: Beyond Journey's End - Frieren The Mage
description:
"I will bring you on with me into the future's light"

Lately I've been watching this beautiful anime and became bewitched by its story and characters. I couldn't resist to bring these beloved characters into the physical realm.

Frieren the mage

I modeled these characters with a lot of love and attention to details. They are designed and scaled to be printed at 1:10 scale, but you can scale them to your liking (or to make them fit your figures collection, if you have one)

I have included a personalized base for each character, featuring a motif for each character (a dungeon's stone floor for frieren, a great hall floor for Himmel, A holy pentagram seal for Heiter and Celtic design for Eisen) I chose a hexagon style in order to organize them together, and I plan to do this for the next figures I make.

These are 3D models designed for printing, textures are not included. Please leave a like and a rating if you enjoy it and post your makes!

This file is for your personal use only, and resale is prohibited.

If you like the models featured here, you can check them out, and some more, here: My creations
printSettings:
These files are optimized for resin printers, but at larger scales can be printed in FDM printers. 

This time I decided to use couplings for joining the pieces, and I used a tolerance of around 0.2mm to account for resin expansion, but it's always best to check your settings to avoid over exposure.

The figures come pre scaled at 1:10, and are about 14cm to 20cm in height (assembled)

title: Frieren: Beyond Journey's End - Übel Staff
description:
This model is designed for Übel cosplay from the manga/anime Frieren: Beyond Journey's End.

This model consists of three main parts, the head, pommel, and pole. The head is cut into 4 parts to fit in a printer with a platform size of 200mm x 200mm x 200mm. Each part is sliced in half for a flat face that can easily lay on the printing bed.

There is a picture with pieces labeled to their file names in the preview section which you can refer to.

There are tunnels with diameters of 10mm punch on the model for alignment and bone installation purposes. You can use anything with a similar dimension for these purposes or print the included "peg" file, a cylinder with a 9.5mm diameter.

Once pieces a and b are printed, glue them back to back using super glue to form a part. Repeat the same for the other pairs and join them afterward.

To bridge the head and pommel. You can print the included "pole", it is 180mm in length. You can print multiple copies of the pole and join them to achieve the desired length. For this purpose, you can also use a ready-made pole with a diameter of not more than 25mm.

For scaling, please use the same scale for all the pieces.

Cheers and Happy Printing!

====UPDATE====
11th April 2024
1. Adding 1a alt, 1b alt, and acc. (Refer to the picture in the preview section for more details)

title: Frieren (Pajama Edition): A thousand years of accumulated sleep for 3D printing!
description:
Lleva a tu vitrina el lado más humano (y perezoso) de nuestra elfa favorita. Esta estatua 3D captura a Frieren en un momento de descanso total, luciendo un tierno conjunto de pijama rosa con detalles de lazos blancos. Es la pieza ideal para romper con la seriedad de los grimorios y las batallas, dándole un toque de dulzura y cotidianidad a tu colección de Frieren: Beyond Journey's End.

¿Por qué este modelo es un "Hechizo de Calidad Superior"?
💎 Escultura de Alta Fidelidad: Cada detalle, desde los sutiles pliegues de la ropa y los elásticos del pantalón hasta sus icónicas coletas y orejas puntiagudas, ha sido modelado con resolución extrema. Olvida los polígonos marcados; este archivo está optimizado para ofrecer superficies ultra-suaves y detalles nítidos en cada impresión.

🛠️ Ingeniería Modular (Split & Keyed): Para que imprimir no sea un rompecabezas mágico, el modelo viene profesionalmente dividido con encastres (keys):

Impresión Optimizada: Los cortes están estratégicamente ubicados para que necesites el mínimo de soportes, protegiendo las zonas más delicadas como el rostro y el cabello.

Pintado Nivel Experto: Al tener la cabeza, el torso, las piernas y la base por separado, podrás pintar cada pieza con total limpieza antes de ensamblar. ¡Lograr el tono rosa perfecto nunca fue tan fácil!

Ensamblaje Perfecto: Las piezas encajan con precisión milimétrica para una estabilidad total.

📦 ¡Versión Full (No Recortada) Incluida!: ¿Tienes una impresora de gran formato o eres de los que prefiere hacer sus propios cortes? ¡Te tenemos cubierto! Se incluye el modelo completo en una sola pieza para que tengas el control absoluto.

🎨 Base de Exhibición Minimalista: Incluye una base circular elegante que resalta la figura sin quitarle protagonismo, dándole ese acabado de estatua de colección profesional.

description:
Incluso una elfa de más de mil años tiene sus momentos de quiebre total. Esta pieza captura la icónica y divertidísima expresión de Frieren llorando de forma desconsolada, una escena que se ha vuelto legendaria entre los fans por su mezcla de ternura y humor. Es la adición perfecta para darle personalidad (y un poco de drama cómico) a tu colección de Frieren: Beyond Journey's End.

¿Por qué este modelo es un "Grimorio de Clase S" para tu impresora?
💎 Calidad de Escultura Superior: El modelo captura cada detalle de la frustración de la elfa: desde sus mejillas sonrojadas y las lágrimas en sus ojos, hasta la tensión en sus manos y el movimiento de sus coletas. Todo ha sido esculpido con una resolución extrema para asegurar superficies lisas y detalles nítidos.

Impresión Optimizada: Los cortes estratégicos minimizan los soportes en el rostro y zonas visibles.

Pintado Facilitado: Podrás pintar el traje, el cabello y la piel por separado para un acabado profesional impecable.

Ensamblaje Perfecto: Las uniones encajan con total exactitud, garantizando una figura sólida y estable.

🎨 Base de Exhibición: Incluye una base circular minimalista que mantiene a Frieren en su pose de rodillas, lista para ser exhibida.

¡No dejes que siga llorando sola! Descarga este modelo hoy mismo y dale un hogar (y quizás un nuevo hechizo raro) a nuestra elfa favorita.

title: Stark Axe - Frieren Beyond Journeys End 3D print model
description:
Starks axe from Frieren: Beyond Journey's End / Sousou no Frieren

This is 3d model (OBJ/STL) of said axe for cosplay purposes, total heigh of it is 148 cm. 

All 3d files are cut and oriented to fit 20cm * 20cm *25 cm 3d printed (eg. it will fit on Ender 3). Lots of parts are oriented with 45 degree angle and will need supports, this is for better part durability while keeping it printable without any complications. There is also a file which contain whole axe as 1 part.

Since it's pretty big, it can be disassembled to 6 pieces for easier travel arrangements. Parts along stick path are screwable, big axe head is hold by metal rod and small axe head by few m3 screws, but everything can be glued if you don't care about that.

There are 5 rar files, simply download "Stark axe (all in one)  - 0.25 inch rod.rar" or "Stark axe (all in one) - 5mm rod.rar" and you should have all what you need. Otherwise you can also download "Stark axe (2 of 2)  - universal parts.rar" and one of "Stark axe (1 of 2)  - 0.25 inch rod parts.rar" / "Stark axe (1 of 2) - 5mm rod parts.rar"

What you will need (optional):

metal rod (for better sturdiness) - the model has 0,5mm clearance for this rod
-  5mm (diameter) x 1m (length) and  5mm (diameter) x 20cm (length)

OR
- 1/4'' (diameter) x 48''  (length) and 1/4'' (diameter) x 8" (length) - this will loose bit of portability since bottom metal and wooden parts will need to be glued together (use 'bottom_metal_stick_0.25x48 inch' part)

OR
-  1/4'' (diameter) x 40'' (length) and 1/4'' (diameter) x 8" (length) - eg. just cut 48'' rod to two (use 'bottom_metal_stick_0.25x40 inch' part)

for small axe-head
- up to 6x 8mm (up to 13mm) m3 screws
- up to 6x heat inserts - M3 x D5.0 x L8.0 (can be shorter) 

Notes:


The cone part on top has 1 full layer that separates bottom and top part of rod slot, it's there for support prevention, just cut it out with exacto knife.
Spike and cone parts can be printed as 1 part, but it's better to print them in 2 parts. If they are printed as 1 part, the spike on top can end up bit misaligned when the cone is tightly screwed in place.
The screw caps are individual files, this is mainly to prevent them from being sanded down when you will work on the spots around their holes. Just glue them in the end of assembly.
Also there are axe head inserts, their purpose is to prevent the slots from cracking during travel.

title: Frieren's staff for cosplay - Frieren: Beyond Journey's End / Sousou no Frieren
description:
A model I made of Frieren’s staff from Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End. Scaled 1:1 for cosplay. The model does not include the handle or the ribbon, so you will need to source these yourself. It’s made so a 1” PVC pipe (with an outer diameter of 33mm / 1-5/16”) can be used for the handle. For the ribbon, I just sewed some fairly cheap red fabric into a strip. 

I wanted the gem parts of my staff made out of red acrylic, but I made the gems completely printable, too (both the small one on the ribbon and the large round one). If you also want to use acrylic or another material for the gems, empty gem holders are included in the “Alternative configurations of components” folder. 

For the sake of printability, I have split up the crescent shaped head. These parts are connected using dowels. While I have included printable versions of these dowels, I personally used 12mm wood and aluminium dowels to make sure the whole thing holds up under stress. However, if you’re not going to be swinging the staff around too enthusiastically, the included printed dowels should work just fine. 

If you experience any issues, or if you have any questions, don’t hesitate to contact me! I’m happy to assist in whatever way I can.
printSettings:
I used 0.2mm layer height on my Ender 3 V3 SE, and printed everything in PLA (though everything should print perfectly fine on a resin printer too). 


15% gyroid infill should suffice for the larger parts
25% gyroid infill is recommended for smaller parts like the dowels


The files in the "Halved components (print in place)" folder can all be printed in place. For the neck and pommel, I would recommend adding some supports if you're not extremely confident in your printer's overhang capabilities. I maintained fairly small printer margins throughout this project, so some sanding may be required to fit pieces together. 

Of course, every printer and filament are different, so take these settings as more of a guideline.

title: Sōsō no Frieren. Seven figures and key chains. Six characters, color and monochrome.
description:
These designs are intended to be made with a 0.2mm nozzle, since their details are very fine. There is a version made for exclusively for a 0.4mm nozzle, which is 3/4 larger.

I have a Hellbot Magna II set up, which uses a two-strand extruder, but it can be done with any printer using the "Pause at Height" setting, in the "Modify Gcode" extension in the "Ultimaker Cura" program.

The bases can be made with any nozzle, for each one I have specified which file to use, always respecting the layer heights for a good finish. I prefer to use the 0.4mm nozzle, since it does not require great detail and they are done in less time.

The calculations are optimized, but you are free to change the heights as you prefer.
printSettings:

Pause Height: 1.52mm, 0.32mm and 0.18mm.

"Frieren Croiser Four Colors Nozzle 0.4mm":

Pause Height: 1.4mm, 0.64mm and 0.36mm

title: Stark (Frieren):The Warrior of the Order of Eisen For 3D Printing!
description:
Lleva a la realidad a Stark, el valiente (aunque a veces asustadizo) guerrero del grupo de Frieren. Este modelo 3D captura toda la potencia del personaje: desde su imponente hacha de combate hasta su característica chaqueta roja y su mirada llena de determinación. Es una pieza de calidad de escultura profesional, diseñada para ser el centro de atención de cualquier vitrina de fans de Frieren: Beyond Journey's End.

¿Por qué este modelo es de nivel "Guerrero Clase S"?
💎 Escultura de Alta Fidelidad (Ultra Detail): Cada centímetro del modelo ha sido trabajado con resolución extrema. Se ha prestado especial atención a la textura metálica y el desgaste del hacha, las costuras y pliegues de su ropa, y el dinamismo de su cabello. Los detalles son tan nítidos que la figura lucirá impresionante incluso en escalas grandes.

🛠️ Ingeniería Modular para una Impresión Épica (Split & Keyed): Para que no tengas que luchar contra los soportes, el modelo incluye una versión profesionalmente dividida con encastres de precisión (keys).

Impresión Optimizada: Los cortes están estratégicamente ubicados para minimizar el uso de soportes en zonas visibles y asegurar superficies suaves.

Facilidad de Pintado: Al tener el hacha, los brazos, el torso y la base por separado, podrás aplicar técnicas de pintura detalladas y efectos de metalizado con total comodidad.

Ensamblaje Robusto: Las uniones (keys) están diseñadas para encajar a la perfección, garantizando una estatua sólida y equilibrada.

📦 Versión Full (No Recortada) Incluida: Para los makers que prefieren gestionar sus propios cortes o tienen impresoras de gran formato, incluimos el Archivo STL de la estatua completa en una sola pieza sólida. Tienes flexibilidad absoluta para tu proyecto.

title: Fern’s Staff – Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End
description:
Cosplay-ready Fern’s Staff from Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End, designed for full-scale accuracy and practical assembly.
This model recreates Fern’s signature staff with smooth, natural wood flow and detailed metallic embellishments that capture the anime’s look in 3D form.

Features:

Screen-accurate proportions and elegant curvature.

7 interlocking wood sections, labeled for easy print order (Parts 1–7).

Separate metal accent pieces (rings, brackets, and band details).

Fully pre-oriented 3MF file with labeled parts and grouped build plates.
printSettings:
Print all wooden segments (Parts 1–7) following ascending order.

Each piece is numbered in the file and on the reference image for clarity.

Print the metal embellishments (rings and brackets) separately in silver, gray, or metallic PLA.

Assembly order:

Start from Part 1 (base) and move upward to Part 7 (tip).

Insert the metal embellishments during assembly — the staff tapers from thin → thick and will not allow sliding the rings on after full assembly.

Optional wrap: Add purple ribbon around the upper joint for show accuracy.

Printer Type: FDM (tested on Bambu Lab X1 Carbon & P1S)
Slicer Profile: Bambu Studio (pre-oriented 3MF provided)
Supports: Not required – all pieces are self-standing and oriented for clean base prints
Full length: ~1500 mm (1:1 cosplay scale)
Optimized for 23"–24" carrying balance, light enough for all-day convention wear.

pre-split 300 mm size scale-Chibi Frieren – Fan Made STL Model for 3D Printing

This fan-made chibi figure of Frieren is inspired by the popular anime Sousou no Frieren (Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End). The model reimagines the elven mage in a cute kawaii chibi style, exaggerating her proportions while keeping her iconic features such as her long elf ears, silver hair and expressive face.

In the story of Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End, Frieren is an ancient elf mage who once traveled with the legendary hero party that defeated the Demon King. Because elves live for centuries, she experiences time very differently than humans. After the death of her companions, Frieren begins a new journey to understand human emotions, memories and the meaning of the time she spent with them.

This fan made chibi interpretation transforms Frieren into a cute collectible anime figure, perfect for fans of the series and collectors of anime statues.

The sculpture was digitally sculpted by Kawaii Studio, focusing on smooth shapes, stylized proportions and clean geometry so the model prints well and looks great once assembled and painted.

The model is split into multiple pieces to make printing, painting and assembly easier.STL FILE FEATURES

title: Lina Inverse (Slayers)
description:
EDIT

In celebration of my  Frieren figure pack 01 release, I am adding a free base for my Lina Inverse model, as well as a version of all files already scaled at 1:10 (Lina is supposed to be around 140cm in height) so that she looks good next to my Frieren figures :)  You'll find it in a zip file, also, I upgraded the pauldron model a little, since it its mesh always looked a little low res. 

You can check my Frieren figures by clicking here:  Frieren: Beyond Journey's End



Lina Inverse, the sorcery genius. Gifted in the magical arts, renowned bandit slayer and feared by friend and foe alike. She packs a serious punch for such a small frame, capable of casting powerful magical spells, such as the Dragon Slave, which can level an entire mountain, the Ragna Blade, a crackling sword made of the dark void which can cut through anything, and the world-ending, Giga Slave, the final magic, which draws upon the power of the Lord of Nightmares herself.

I designed this print for all fans whom, like me, are unable to get their hands on those sweet official figures. Her look comes directly from her latest anime incarnation (Slayers Evolution-R) as I liked the design of her pauldrons. everything else is more or less the same as it was since Slayers Next. Maybe, in the future, I'll do another one in her "Encounter in Atessa" costume.

I used my first gen Anycubic Photon, using Wanhao white resin (5), with a layer height of 0.025mm. I made it in several pieces to make it easier to print in a small resin printer like mine, and still managed to get a decent sized figure (I printed it at 70% of the scale for an approximate height of 13cm) but you can print it bigger if you like, I think I made it detailed enough to look good-ish at larger scales.

(Lina Inverse is a creation of Hajime Kanzaka, Illustrated by Rui Ariazumi)

title: Frieren: Beyond Journey's End Chibi Frieren Kawaii Studio
description:
Frieren Chibi Figure – Frieren: Beyond Journey's End STL 3D Print Statue

This 3D model features Frieren, the iconic elven mage from the anime Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End (Sousou no Frieren), recreated as an adorable chibi anime figure designed for 3D printing.

The sculpture captures Frieren in a dramatic and expressive moment, stylized in a kawaii chibi / super-deformed style while keeping her recognizable features: her long elf ears, silver hair, and her emotional expression. The exaggerated proportions give the figure a collectible look similar to anime statues and chibi figures.

In the lore of Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End, Frieren is a powerful elf mage who was part of the legendary hero party that defeated the Demon King. Because elves live for centuries, Frieren experiences time differently than humans. After the death of her former companions, she begins a new journey to better understand human emotions, memories, and the meaning of the time she spent with them.

This chibi version of Frieren captures the emotional contrast of the character, transforming her into a cute collectible anime figure perfect for fans of the series.

The model was digitally sculpted by our independent studio, GK Studio, focusing on clean shapes, smooth surfaces, and balanced proportions so the figure prints well and looks great once assembled and painted.

The model is split into multiple parts to make printing easier and to allow better painting and assembly.

Perfect for:

• Anime figure collectors
• Fans of Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End
• 3D printing enthusiasts
• Miniature painters
• Anime statue collectors
printSettings:
Recommended printing type

Resin printing (SLA / MSLA / DLP) for best detail.

FDM printing is also possible using fine layer settings.

Model size

Approximately 16 cm tall when assembled

Recommended settings

Layer height
0.05 mm – 0.03 mm (resin)

Infill
100% (resin)

Supports
Required for some parts

Compatible materials

• Resin
• PLA
• ABS
• PETG

Post-processing recommended

• Remove supports
• Light sanding if needed
• Apply primer
• Paint using acrylic miniature paints

title: Himmel the Hero: The Remembrance of Flowers
description:
Este modelo 3D inmortaliza a Himmel el Héroe, el legendario líder del grupo de aventureros de la serie Sousou no Frieren. Diseñada con un delicado estilo Funko Pop!, la figura presenta a Himmel en una pose solemne y heroica, portando su espada y vistiendo su característica capa, pero con el detalle más significativo de su historia: la corona de flores que representa el vínculo eterno con sus compañeros.

Es una pieza cargada de simbolismo, ideal para fans que buscan capturar la esencia melancólica y esperanzadora de la serie. La escala está pensada para resaltar la textura de las flores y la fluidez de la capa, convirtiéndola en un objeto de decoración perfecto para cualquier estantería, escritorio o vitrina de colección.

🌸 Simbolismo Épico: Captura el momento más recordado del héroe, uniendo su faceta guerrera con su lado más humano y sensible.
🔧 Alta Definición: Archivos STL y OBJ optimizados para capturar cada pétalo de la corona y los pliegues de la capa con precisión.
📦 Licencia Comercial: Como parte del catálogo de Studio Suárez, puedes imprimir y vender las copias físicas de este modelo (no está permitida la reventa de los archivos digitales).
🎓 Sello de Autor: Al adquirir este diseño, apoyas el trabajo de un desarrollador chileno que fusiona la ingeniería informática con la pasión por el diseño 3D.
printSettings:
Impresora: FDM (como se muestra en la imagen) o Resina (SLA).
Filamento: PLA Teal o Cian (como en la muestra) para un look místico, o blanco para facilitar la pintura posterior.
Altura de capa: 0.12 mm. Es fundamental para que los detalles de las flores en la corona y los ojos del personaje salgan definidos.
Relleno: 10–15% (Patrón Giroide).
Soportes: Sí, son necesarios. Se recomiendan soportes tipo "árbol" (tree supports) para la parte inferior de la capa, la empuñadura de la espada y los pétalos exteriores de la corona.
Postprocesado: Se sugiere un retiro cuidadoso de soportes en la zona de la cabeza para no dañar las flores. Un acabado con pintura en tonos pastel (azul claro para el cabello y blanco/púrpura para las flores) hará que la pieza destaque de forma espectacular.
